steps.title: Identify Text 2's counter to Text 1's interpretation
steps.content: Text 2 directly challenges the conclusion that magpies are attempting to help by offering an alternative: the magpies might simply be curious about the equipment's novelty, pecking at it without intent to assist.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
D correctly identifies that the behavior doesn't require assuming attempted assistance. A misidentifies the source of novelty, C bases objections on frequency rather than interpretation, and B contradicts both texts by proposing self-benefit.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
